devarchitect — Tasks 5 to 8

Task 5 — `stack` command

After the project vision and feasibility are captured, the developer needs to lock in their technology choices. The stack command captures what technologies will be used and why — so any new developer joining knows exactly what the project is built with and can set up their environment immediately.

This is the third layer of engineering memory devarchitect stores.

Prerequisites

  • .devarchitect/vision.json
  • .devarchitect/analysis.json

Example Output

{
  "techStack": {
    "frontend": "React",
    "backend": "Node.js",
    "database": "MongoDB",
    "deploymentPlatform": "AWS"
  }
}

Task 6 — `roadmap` command

The roadmap command captures the execution plan — how the project will be built phase by phase. This is the most important command after init because it gives any developer a clear picture of where the project is going and what needs to be done next.

This command introduces a new concept — looping questions based on user input.

Prerequisites

  • .devarchitect/vision.json
  • .devarchitect/analysis.json
  • .devarchitect/techstack.json

Example Output

{
  "phases": [
    {
      "phase": 1,
      "name": "Core CLI",
      "milestones": "Build init, export, analyze commands"
    },
    {
      "phase": 2,
      "name": "Dashboard",
      "milestones": "Build express server and react UI"
    }
  ],
  "createdAt": "2026-04-06T09:12:39.664Z"
}

Task 7 — `decision` command

This is the most unique command in devarchitect. Every other command runs once and overwrites its JSON file. The decision command runs many times — every time an important architectural decision is made — and APPENDS to decisions.json instead of overwriting it.

This builds up a log of every decision ever made in the project, with reasoning and timestamp for each.

Prerequisites

  • .devarchitect/vision.json

Example Output

[
  {
    "decision": "Use JSON files instead of a database",
    "reason": "Lightweight, no setup, readable by humans",
    "alternatives": "SQLite, MongoDB",
    "decidedAt": "2026-04-06T09:12:39.664Z"
  },
  {
    "decision": "Use Commander.js for CLI parsing",
    "reason": "Most popular, well documented, simple API",
    "alternatives": "yargs, meow",
    "decidedAt": "2026-04-07T11:30:00.000Z"
  }
]

Task 8 — `progress` command

Progress is the living state of the project — it gets updated regularly as work gets done. It tells any developer looking at the project exactly where things stand right now: which phase is active, which milestones are done, and what is blocking forward movement.

This command introduces reading from roadmap.json to show the developer their own phases as choices — so they do not have to remember or retype them.

Prerequisites

  • .devarchitect/vision.json
  • .devarchitect/roadmap.json

Example Output

{
  "phase": "2",
  "milestones": "Build express server and react UI",
  "createdAt": "2026-04-10T10:30:00.000Z"
}
